NJIT's Perspective on AI Usage in Teaching/Learning | Emerging Technologies

artifacts.claimText: NJIT states that students must follow instructor expectations, uphold academic honesty when using AI tools, and not use AI tools to conduct or support cheating or plagiarism.

artifacts.claimText: NJIT says that if students have not received explicit instructor authorization to use AI, submitting AI-generated work may constitute a violation of the Code of Student Conduct and Academic Integrity policies.

citation.citationTitle: Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence | Dean of Students and Campus Life

artifacts.claimText: NJIT guidance says instructors have autonomy to develop course-specific AI policies, and students should check the syllabus and ask the instructor when unsure whether generative AI use is permitted.

Approved as a narrow staged candidate using the source's cautious may-constitute language.
Approved as a teaching-guidance candidate limited to course-specific instructor autonomy and syllabus checking.

artifacts.title: New Jersey Institute of Technology (NJIT) AI Policy Evidence Staging
artifacts.summary: Staged official NJIT evidence covers student academic integrity expectations for AI, course-level instructor discretion, security/data vetting for university-wide licensed AI tools, confidential-data cautions, and listed AI tool availability.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged candidates only and are not canonical public facts until promoted through the review process.
Nexus knowledge-base details linked from NJIT pages were not crawled because this run used only public official pages without login or access-control bypass.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
